question: Suppose a model tries to identify the backpack only by its dark color and floor-level location. Which additional feature provides the strongest evidence that it is the same moved object across views rather than just another dark object near the door?
Which pair would be the worst over-merge if a model ignored both material and object function and only tracked 'small thing near papers or labels'?

Which of the following is the strongest example of a question where a careless model might overconfidently answer 'same object' even though the evidence only supports 'visually similar and possibly related'?
Which object is most clearly identified by a combination of color, shape, and attachment state rather than by location alone?
Which object is best described as remaining in the archive but becoming harder to see because of position and occlusion rather than fully disappearing?

Which object is best supported as having changed from 'not visible in the later desk close-up' to 'present elsewhere nearby' WITHOUT relying on any assumption that two similar blue objects are identical?
Which statement about the transparent plastic bag is correct?
In the first living-room image, which of the three objects on the coffee table was closest to the front-right corner of the table surface?
Between the first and next-morning living-room images, which exact seat or side of the dining setup had the gray hoodie in the earlier image?
In the bedroom-desk image, which item sat immediately to the right of the closed black laptop?
On the hallway-closet middle shelf, which item was nearest to the green rectangular card?
In the later kitchen image from the afternoon, was the faint circular stain located left of the pill container, right of it, directly in front of it or directly behind of it.?
In the entryway-bench close-up, was the upright blue umbrella leaning closer to the bench frame, the wall, or the nearest pair of shoes?
In the later living-room image from S6, on which side of the TV remote was the round coaster located?
In the first fossil-room image, which right-side item was closest to the front edge of the display surface: the blue shell-shaped object or the black tag marked “C-1127”?
In the lower-front fossil-room crop, how much of the blue shell-shaped object was visible to the right of the torn label: only a narrow edge, roughly half, most of the object or whole of the object?

Between the first fossil-room image and the later fossil-room image, did the green drawstring pouch appear remained unchanged, centered directly behind the rock, shifted slightly left, or shifted slightly right relative to the fossil slab?
In the maritime gallery case, was the hanging tag “A-209” positioned closer to the blue fish-shaped object or closer to the brass dial instrument?
On the restoration table, was the metal tool more horizontally aligned with the compass, slightly above it, or slightly below it?

In the restoration-table right-side close-up, was the red-handled brass seal stamp placed nearer the front edge of the table than the drafting tool, farther back than it, or at roughly the same depth?
In the storage prep room, was the folded dark green velvet pouch closer to which one ?
In the next-morning fossil-room image, was the hanging tag “A-209” vertically higher than the top of the blue fish-shaped object, lower than it, or overlapping its vertical level?
In the later restoration-room image, was the small corked glass jar of sand positioned closer to the compass or closer to the blank rectangular label?

In the maritime gallery close-up, did the chipped-corner blank label sit more directly in front of the round brass object, more directly in front of the blue ceramic fish, or between them with no clear front alignment to either?
Which room contains the strongest cluster of distractors that could fool a model relying only on rough category labels like 'brass object', 'blue object', and 'blank label'?
In the first main-bench image, which item was closest to the front-left corner of the yellow rack?
In the closer crop of the front bench area, was the missing front-left red-capped tube positioned nearer to the front edge of the bench or nearer to the centerline of the rack?
In the later main-bench image, was the strip of small clear tubes positioned directly behind the yellow rack, offset to its left, or offset to its right?
In the pipette-area image, was the black clipped label closer to which one.
On the first cold-room shelf image, was the loose barcode label placed more in front of the yellow rack, more beside the folded white towels, or midway between those regions?
In the lower cold-room shelf image, was the strip of five small clear microtubes aligned more closely with the large blue-capped vial on the left or with the metal tweezers on the right?
In the centrifuge-station wide image, which of the two labeled sample vials in the foam rack sat nearer the strip of microtubes: the amber-liquid vial or the pink-liquid vial?
In the close-up of the right side of the centrifuge station, did the diagonal tweezers point more toward the microtube strip, more toward the sample rack, or more toward the lower edge of the frame?
Comparing the main-bench strip and the centrifuge-station strip, was the blue-filled tube in the same ordinal slot position within the strip, or not?
In the first main prop-table image, which item sat closest to the front-right corner of the table surface ?
In the left-half prop-table crop, was the brass pocket watch placed closer to the singed-edged parchment or closer to the pearl necklace?
In the later prop-table image after rehearsal traffic, was the remaining red fan aligned more closely with the center hat, the front-edge 'CUE' card area, or the perfume-bottle cluster?
In the wings-area image, was the gold-banded black hat positioned more toward the front edge of the stool seat, more toward the back edge, or centered on the seat?
At the quick-change station, was the blue bottle placed nearer to which one?
In the closer quick-change counter crop, was the wooden cane leaning with its handle end nearer the watch side of the counter or nearer the mirror side?
In the costume-rack zone, was the blue glass bottle placed directly behind the red folding fan, offset to one side of it, or separated from it by the hat?
In the final prop-table image, was the brass pocket watch positioned more to the left of the hat, more in front of it, or more to the right of it?
Where is the small red USB drive in the most recent archive images?
In the most recent images, where is the small brass compass that was originally on the left side of the fossil display case?
Where is the gold pocket watch with the engraved star lid currently located in the backstage area?
Where are the bent-tip forceps that were originally on the main lab bench?
What identification tag number is currently displayed in the fossil room case?
Comparing the storage cabinet's middle shelf between its first appearance and the later rearrangement, in which direction did the gray storage box shift?
How did the spatial arrangement of items on the bedroom desk change between its first and most recent recorded states?
In the most recent cabinet image, how has the red USB drive's position on the middle shelf changed compared to its initial placement there?
When the two truck routes finally stop looking alike, what is the giveaway on the under-bridge one?
After the daytime crosswalk clears, what does the car do next?
What is above the road on the calm under-bridge night run?
On the office run that keeps restarting, what shows up later that is missing from the easier daytime office route?
Which office run is the one with the gray van on the right rather than the pale-blue car beside it?
Near the end, what finally separates those two truck streets?
Which of those two green-light runs ends with a small vehicle crossing ahead?
Of those same two green-light runs, which one bends farther right near the end?
After the pedestrians clear, what does the busy night crossing do that the calm under-bridge night run does not?
Later on the daytime crosswalk run, what do you see after the stop?
Before the tower-side bottleneck clears, where does the opening finally appear?
Which office route later drifts left instead of staying broad after the light?
Once those two daytime problem runs are moving again, what is the real difference between them?
When I go back to the office notes later, which combination tells me I am looking at the easy daytime baseline?
Which office route starts behind a light-colored car and later leans left through the greener stretch?
One night run starts to look easier, but it never really does. Which earlier night run is it closest to?
Which road is the one that slips around the tight spot instead of turning off after the wait?
If the car keeps following the same truck through the shop-lined block, which other truck route is it not?
Near the end of the green-light corner, where does the small delivery vehicle pass relative to the white SUV?
In the later office view, what detail separates the restart-prone office route from the calmer daytime one?
Once the daytime crosswalk clears, which side picks up the fence as the car turns away from the main road?
Which of these runs is the only one that first gets around an obstacle on the left?
On which run does a delivery tricycle cross in front of a stopped white SUV near the end?
Which run opens left for a moment and then lines up again near the end?
Which run later shows the dark parked car at the side instead of a truck still centered ahead?
After movement starts again in these runs, how many different next steps do you actually have to remember?
At night, which route still feels tight on the left even after the light changes?
At night, which run still feels squeezed from one side long after the light changes?
